message: Fix install ref parsing bootstrap regressions

id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription - features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[] - integrationLevel, category, status ## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT) ### 1.1 Primary Documentation - `cat /dp/repo_updater/README.md` - Read full README - Check for repo configuration docs, batch operation specs ### 1.2 Code Investigation - Launch code investigation agent to understand: - Repo discovery mechanism (/dp/ scanning) - Batch update operations (pull, fetch, status) - Conflict handling strategy - Parallel vs sequential processing - Status reporting format ### 1.3 CLI Command Verification ```bash # Verify each documented command works ru --help 2>&1 | head -20 ru status --help 2>&1 | head -10 ru pull --help 2>&1 | head -10 # Test actual functionality ru status 2>&1 | head -10 ``` ### 1.4 External Context Search - `/xf search 'repo updater OR ru OR batch git'` - Twitter archive - `cass search 'ru repo update' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions ### 1.5 Project State Review - Check beads in /dp/repo_updater/.beads/ - Review recent commits: `cd /dp/repo_updater && git log --oneline -20` ## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING) ### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification Document findings for each area: - [ ] Repo discovery mechanism - [ ] Batch operations available - [ ] Conflict handling approach - [ ] Parallel processing capability - [ ] Status reporting format ### 2.2 Synergy Verification Cross-reference these tools actually integrate: - [ ] gh - GitHub CLI operations - [ ] mail - status notifications - [ ] ntm - coordinated updates ### 2.3 Performance Verification ```bash # Count repos managed ls -d /dp/*/.git 2>/dev/null | wc -l echo "Total repos in /dp/" # Test status speed time ru status --quiet 2>/dev/null | head -5 ``` ## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS) ### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts Update ru entry with VERIFIED information: - `tagline`: Batch repo maintenance - `description`: Update mechanism - `deepDescription`: How batch operations work - `features`: Verified capabilities - `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work - `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations ### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with: - `briefDescription`: Technical summary - `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities - `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples ## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES) ### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation ```bash cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 ``` ### 4.2 Unit Tests ```bash # Test ru entry structure node -e " const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel'); const ru = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ru'); console.log('Testing ru entry...'); console.assert(ru, 'ru entry exists'); console.assert(ru.features?.length > 0, 'has features'); console.assert(ru.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands'); console.log('All assertions passed'); " ``` ### 4.3 E2E Test: Batch Operations ```bash #\!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail LOG=/tmp/ru-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log echo "=== RU E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG # Test 1: Status all repos echo "Test 1: Status check..." | tee -a $LOG ru status 2>&1 | head -15 | tee -a $LOG # Test 2: List repos echo "Test 2: List repos..." | tee -a $LOG ru list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No list command" # Test 3: Dry run pull (if available) echo "Test 3: Dry run..." | tee -a $LOG ru pull --dry-run 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No dry-run option" echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG echo "Log: $LOG" ``` ### 4.4 Content Diff Verification ```bash echo "=== Changes Made ===" diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true ``` ## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE) ### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail) ```bash cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state" ``` ### 5.2 Sync Changes ```bash br update bd-1j4m --status done br sync --flush-only ``` ### 5.3 Final Verification - [ ] All tests pass - [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors - [ ] Batch operations documented correctly - [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:32.106227619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:45.634375752Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:45.634346477Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1j8r","title":"Add key auth vs password explanation for reconnection","description":"# Task: Add Key Auth vs Password Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np) ## Description The reconnect command uses `-i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519` which is NEW. Users don't understand they're now using key authentication instead of password. They might wonder "where do I enter my password?" or panic when they're not prompted for one. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add explanation above the SSH command: \`\`\`tsx

Notice something different?

This SSH command uses your SSH key (the -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 part) instead of a password. The installer set this up for you!

No password needed! When you run this command, you'll connect immediately without typing a password. That's the magic of SSH keys — more secure AND more convenient.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the change from password to key auth - [ ] Reassures that no password prompt is expected - [ ] Briefly mentions this is more secure - [ ] Appears before the SSH command - [ ] Friendly, not technical tone ## UI/UX Notes - Frame it as a positive upgrade, not a change to learn - "Magic" language makes it feel like a benefit - Keep it brief — users are eager to reconnect","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:42:42.043314Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T19:28:00.229279Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T19:28:00.229279Z","close_reason":"Implemented key-auth (no password) explanation above reconnect SSH command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-1knvb","title":"Fix install.sh bootstrap and ref parsing regressions","description":"Fresh-eyes peer-code audit found install.sh failed in stripped envs before HOME repair, --checksums-ref was order-dependent with --ref, and equals-form empty ref flags were accepted. Fix landed in this session with focused regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:37:15.345298514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:37:27.836015Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:37:27.835755514Z","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh stripped-env bootstrap, explicit checksum-ref parsing, and empty ref flag validation; added focused regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1kr5","title":"Fix session sanitizer to redact quoted secrets","description":"Session export sanitizer misses common patterns like password="secret" and JSON "password": "secret" because generic key/value regex requires unquoted values. Expand the generic key/value redaction regex (sed + jq) to allow optional quotes around the secret value.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T05:00:59.308305Z","updated_at":"2025-12-29T05:04:26.675317Z","closed_at":"2025-12-29T05:04:26.675317Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1lug","title":"Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls","description":"## Overview Implement exponential backoff for GitHub API rate limit errors to prevent installation failures during peak times. ## Current Problem - GitHub API has rate limits (60/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr with token) - Installation fetches many resources (releases, checksums) - Rate limit errors cause hard failures - No retry mechanism ## Proposed Behavior - Detect 403 rate limit responses - Implement exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s - Show user-friendly message during wait - Fall back to cached versions if available - Suggest `gh auth login` if unauthenticated ## Implementation Details ```bash github_fetch_with_backoff() { local url="$1" max_retries=5 delay=1 for ((i=1; i<=max_retries; i++)); do response=$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o /tmp/response "$url") if [[ "$response" == "200" ]]; then cat /tmp/response; return 0 elif [[ "$response" == "403" ]]; then log_warn "Rate limited, waiting ${delay}s (attempt $i/$max_retries)" sleep "$delay" delay=$((delay * 2)) else return 1 fi done return 1 } ``` ## Rate Limit Detection - HTTP 403 with "rate limit" in body - X-RateLimit-Remaining header = 0 - X-RateLimit-Reset header for exact wait time ## User Communication ``` ⏳ GitHub rate limit reached. Waiting 8s before retry (3/5)... 💡 Tip: Run 'gh auth login' for higher rate limits ``` ## Test Plan - [ ] Test backoff timing is correct - [ ] Test max retries respected - [ ] Test successful retry after limit clears - [ ] Mock test for rate limit response handling - [ ] Test fallback to cached versions ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/security.sh (fetch_with_checksum uses curl) - New: scripts/lib/github_api.sh (GitHub-specific helpers)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:55.353096702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:15:03.879490198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:15:03.879471172Z","close_reason":"Implemented github_api.sh with exponential backoff for rate limits. Integrated into security.sh. Added unit tests (12/12 passing). Features: HTTP 403/429 detection, X-RateLimit-Remaining header support, user-friendly wait messages with gh auth tip, configurable backoff (1s-60s).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lug","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:08.229835256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-1ngy","title":"Deep exploration: PT (Process Triage)","description":"## Goal Perform deep exploration of PT (Process Triage) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing. ## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL) ### 0.1 Tool Existence Check ```bash # Verify PT installation [[ -d /dp/process_triage ]] && echo "PASS: pt repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: pt repo missing"; exit 1; } command -v pt &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: pt command available" || { echo "FAIL: pt not in PATH"; exit 1; } # Check for root/sudo availability (often needed for process management) [[ $(id -u) -eq 0 ]] && echo "INFO: Running as root" || echo "INFO: Running as user (some features may need sudo)" ``` ### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State) ```bash SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/pt-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR" ``` ### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference Content must match FlywheelTool interface: - id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription - features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[] - integrationLevel, category, status ## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT) ### 1.1 Primary Documentation - `cat /dp/process_triage/README.md` - Read full README - Check for triage algorithm docs, priority rules ### 1.2 Code Investigation - Launch code investigation agent to understand: - Process discovery mechanism - Triage algorithm (how it prioritizes) - Resource consumption analysis - Kill/throttle recommendations - Integration with system monitoring ### 1.3 CLI Command Verification ```bash # Verify each documented command works pt --help 2>&1 | head -20 pt triage --help 2>&1 | head -10 pt top --help 2>&1 | head -10 # Test actual functionality pt status 2>&1 | head -10 ``` ### 1.4 External Context Search - `/xf search 'process triage OR pt OR resource hog'` - Twitter archive - `cass search 'pt process kill' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions ### 1.5 Project State Review - Check beads in /dp/process_triage/.beads/ - Review recent commits: `cd /dp/process_triage && git log --oneline -20` ## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING) ### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification Document findings for each area: - [ ] Process discovery mechanism - [ ] Triage algorithm and scoring - [ ] Resource metrics tracked (CPU, mem, IO, FD) - [ ] Recommendation system (kill, throttle, ignore) - [ ] Output formats (human, robot) ### 2.2 Synergy Verification Cross-reference these tools actually integrate: - [ ] srps - resource protection integration - [ ] rch - build process monitoring - [ ] mail - alert notifications - [ ] ntm - agent process coordination ### 2.3 Algorithm Verification ```bash # Run triage and check output format pt triage --robot 2>&1 | head -20 # Check scoring metrics pt metrics 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No metrics command" # Verify resource tracking pt top --limit 5 2>&1 | head -10 ``` ## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS) ### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts Update pt entry with VERIFIED information: - `tagline`: Process triage and prioritization - `description`: Resource consumption analysis - `deepDescription`: How triage algorithm works - `features`: Verified capabilities - `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work - `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations ### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with: - `briefDescription`: Technical summary - `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities - `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples ## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES) ### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation ```bash cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 ``` ### 4.2 Unit Tests ```bash # Test pt entry structure node -e " const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel'); const pt = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'pt'); console.log('Testing pt entry...'); console.assert(pt, 'pt entry exists'); console.assert(pt.features?.length > 0, 'has features'); console.assert(pt.connectsTo?.includes('srps'), 'connects to srps'); console.log('All assertions passed'); " ``` ### 4.3 E2E Test: Process Triage ```bash #\!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail LOG=/tmp/pt-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log echo "=== PT E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG # Test 1: Status echo "Test 1: Status..." | tee -a $LOG pt status 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG # Test 2: Triage echo "Test 2: Triage..." | tee -a $LOG pt triage --limit 5 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG # Test 3: Robot output echo "Test 3: Robot mode..." | tee -a $LOG pt triage --robot --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG echo "Log: $LOG" ``` ### 4.4 Content Diff Verification ```bash echo "=== Changes Made ===" diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true ``` ## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE) ### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail) ```bash cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state" ``` ### 5.2 Sync Changes ```bash br update bd-1ngy --status done br sync --flush-only ``` ### 5.3 Final Verification - [ ] All tests pass - [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors - [ ] Triage algorithm documented correctly - [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (pt->srps and srps->pt) - [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:48.246583962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:17:32.519414898Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:17:32.519387206Z","close_reason":"Deep exploration complete. Updated flywheel.ts and tldr-content.ts with verified PT information: four-state Bayesian classification (Useful/Bad/Abandoned/Zombie), evidence-based posterior inference with confidence levels, protected process lists, identity validation, staged kill signals, blast radius assessment, agent/robot mode with safety gates, session bundles (.ptb). Fixed install command (curl not cargo). Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -63,17 +63,22 @@ ACFS_REF="${ACFS_REF:-main}"
 ACFS_REF_INPUT="$ACFS_REF"
 # Checksums ref defaults to ACFS_REF_INPUT, but pinned tags/SHAs fall back to main
 # to avoid stale checksums for fast-moving upstream installers.
-ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF:-}"
+_ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_FROM_ENV="${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF:-}"
+ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=false
+ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="$_ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_FROM_ENV"
 if [[ -z "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF" ]]; then
     if [[ "$ACFS_REF_INPUT" =~ ^v[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){1,2}([.-][A-Za-z0-9]+)*$ ]] || [[ "$ACFS_REF_INPUT" =~ ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$ ]]; then
         ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="main"
     else
         ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="$ACFS_REF_INPUT"
     fi
+else
+    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=true
 fi
+unset _ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_FROM_ENV
 ACFS_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${ACFS_REPO_OWNER}/${ACFS_REPO_NAME}/${ACFS_REF}"
 ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${ACFS_REPO_OWNER}/${ACFS_REPO_NAME}/${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF}"
-export ACFS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW ACFS_VERSION
+export ACFS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT ACFS_VERSION
 export CHECKSUMS_FILE="${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_YAML:-${CHECKSUMS_FILE:-}}"
 ACFS_COMMIT_SHA=""       # Short SHA for display (12 chars)
 ACFS_COMMIT_SHA_FULL=""  # Full SHA for pinning resume scripts (40 chars)
@@ -89,9 +94,15 @@ if [[ -n "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" && -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" ]]; then
     SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 fi
 
-# Early PATH setup: ensure ~/.local/bin is available for native installers (e.g., Claude Code)
-# This is critical because the Claude native installer puts the binary at ~/.local/bin/claude
-export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
+# Early PATH setup: ensure ~/.local/bin is available for native installers
+# when HOME is present, without assuming stripped environments already set it.
+_ACFS_EARLY_PATH="${PATH:-/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin}"
+if [[ -n "${HOME:-}" ]]; then
+    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$_ACFS_EARLY_PATH"
+else
+    export PATH="$_ACFS_EARLY_PATH"
+fi
+unset _ACFS_EARLY_PATH
 
 acfs_early_system_binary_path() {
     local name="${1:-}"
@@ -1025,6 +1036,13 @@ generate_resume_hint() {
         resume_args+=(--ref "$ACFS_REF_INPUT")
     fi
 
+    # Preserve an explicit checksum metadata ref across resume commands. The
+    # default checksum ref is derived from --ref, so only include this when it
+    # was deliberately pinned by CLI or environment.
+    if [[ "${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT:-false}" == "true" && -n "${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF:-}" ]]; then
+        resume_args+=(--checksums-ref "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF")
+    fi
+
     # Add skip flags that were used
     [[ "${SKIP_POSTGRES:-false}" == "true" ]] && resume_args+=(--skip-postgres)
     [[ "${SKIP_VAULT:-false}" == "true" ]] && resume_args+=(--skip-vault)
@@ -1183,6 +1201,19 @@ trap '_acfs_signal_handler HUP'  HUP
 # ============================================================
 # Parse arguments
 # ============================================================
+acfs_require_ref_arg_value() {
+    local flag="$1"
+    local value="${2:-}"
+    local example="$3"
+
+    if [[ -z "$value" || "$value" == -* ]]; then
+        log_fatal "$flag requires a ref (e.g., $example)"
+    fi
+    if [[ "$value" == *$'\n'* || "$value" == *$'\r'* ]]; then
+        log_fatal "$flag requires a single-line ref"
+    fi
+}
+
 parse_args() {
     while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
         case $1 in
@@ -1271,17 +1302,17 @@ parse_args() {
                 ;;
             --checksums-ref|--checksums-ref=*)
                 if [[ "$1" == "--checksums-ref" ]]; then
-                    if [[ -z "${2:-}" || "$2" == -* ]]; then
-                        log_fatal "--checksums-ref requires a ref (e.g., --checksums-ref main)"
-                    fi
+                    acfs_require_ref_arg_value "--checksums-ref" "${2:-}" "--checksums-ref main"
                     ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="$2"
                     shift 2
                 else
                     ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="${1#*=}"
+                    acfs_require_ref_arg_value "--checksums-ref" "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF" "--checksums-ref=main"
                     shift
                 fi
+                ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=true
                 ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${ACFS_REPO_OWNER}/${ACFS_REPO_NAME}/${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF}"
-                export ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW
+                export ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT
                 ;;
             --pin-ref|--confirm-ref)
                 # Print resolved SHA and pinned command, then exit
@@ -1293,25 +1324,28 @@ parse_args() {
                 # bind to curl, not bash: ACFS_REF=v1 curl ... | bash
                 # doesn't propagate to the bash process)
                 if [[ "$1" == "--ref" ]]; then
-                    if [[ -z "${2:-}" || "$2" == -* ]]; then
-                        log_fatal "--ref requires a git ref (branch, tag, or SHA)"
-                    fi
+                    acfs_require_ref_arg_value "--ref" "${2:-}" "--ref main"
                     ACFS_REF="$2"
                     shift 2
                 else
                     ACFS_REF="${1#*=}"
+                    acfs_require_ref_arg_value "--ref" "$ACFS_REF" "--ref=main"
                     shift
                 fi
                 ACFS_REF_INPUT="$ACFS_REF"
                 ACFS_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${ACFS_REPO_OWNER}/${ACFS_REPO_NAME}/${ACFS_REF}"
-                # Recalculate checksums ref for the new ref
-                if [[ "$ACFS_REF" =~ ^v[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){1,2}([.-][A-Za-z0-9]+)*$ ]] || [[ "$ACFS_REF" =~ ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$ ]]; then
-                    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="main"
-                else
-                    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="$ACFS_REF"
+                # Recalculate checksums ref for the new install ref unless the
+                # user explicitly pinned checksum metadata with --checksums-ref
+                # or ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF.
+                if [[ "${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
+                    if [[ "$ACFS_REF" =~ ^v[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){1,2}([.-][A-Za-z0-9]+)*$ ]] || [[ "$ACFS_REF" =~ ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$ ]]; then
+                        ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="main"
+                    else
+                        ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF="$ACFS_REF"
+                    fi
                 fi
                 ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${ACFS_REPO_OWNER}/${ACFS_REPO_NAME}/${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF}"
-                export ACFS_REF ACFS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW
+                export ACFS_REF ACFS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF ACFS_CHECKSUMS_RAW ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT
                 ;;
             --skip-ubuntu-upgrade)
                 # Skip automatic Ubuntu version upgrade (nb4)

@@ -82,12 +82,22 @@ extract_normalize_read_only_modes_function() {
     sed -n '/^normalize_read_only_modes()/,/^}$/p' "$REPO_ROOT/install.sh"
 }
 
+extract_parse_args_function() {
+    sed -n '/^parse_args()/,/^}$/p' "$REPO_ROOT/install.sh"
+}
+
+extract_ref_arg_value_helper() {
+    sed -n '/^acfs_require_ref_arg_value()/,/^}$/p' "$REPO_ROOT/install.sh"
+}
+
 # Actually, let's just define our test environment and source install.sh functions
 setup_test_env() {
     # Reset all variables to defaults
     SCRIPT_DIR=""
     ACFS_COMMIT_SHA_FULL=""
     ACFS_REF_INPUT=""
+    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF=""
+    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=false

+test_checksums_ref_survives_ref_parse_order() {
+    setup_parse_args_env
+    parse_args --checksums-ref explicit-checksums --ref feature-branch --print-plan
+
+    if [[ "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF" != "explicit-checksums" ]]; then
+        log "  Expected explicit checksums ref to survive later --ref, got: $ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF"
+        return 1
+    fi
+    if [[ "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT" != "true" ]]; then
+        log "  Expected ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=true, got: $ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    setup_parse_args_env
+    parse_args --ref feature-branch --checksums-ref explicit-checksums --print-plan
+
+    if [[ "$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF" != "explicit-checksums" ]]; then
+        log "  Expected explicit checksums ref to apply after --ref, got: $ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
+
+test_custom_checksums_ref_resume_hint() {
+    setup_test_env
+    SCRIPT_DIR="/some/local/path"
+    ACFS_REF_INPUT="feature-branch"
+    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF='checksums;touch /tmp/acfs-pwned #'
+    ACFS_CHECKSUMS_REF_EXPLICIT=true
+
+    local result
+    result=$(generate_resume_hint "" "")
+
+    if [[ "$result" != *"--checksums-ref checksums\\;touch"* ]]; then
+        log "  Expected shell-escaped --checksums-ref in resume hint, got: $result"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$result" == *"checksums;touch"* ]]; then
+        log "  Expected checksum ref metacharacters to be escaped, got: $result"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
